event: Interior of a School in Cairo
symbols: books and writing tablets — the transmission of sacred knowledge across generations
ornate architectural decoration — the reverence for learning spaces in Near Eastern culture
teacher's elevated position — the authority of religious instruction
gathered students — the communal nature of scriptural education
description: An Orientalist watercolor depicting the interior of a traditional school (madrasa or kuttab) in Cairo. An elderly teacher or sheikh sits at the right side of the room, wearing a white turban and robes, overseeing a group of young students seated on richly patterned carpets and cushions on the floor. The students hold writing boards or manuscripts. The interior features elaborately carved wooden screens, ornate Islamic geometric patterns on the walls, and warm golden light streaming through a window on the left.

_meta.title: Interior of a School in Cairo
